isolation.credentials: none — no pre-existing test user; sign-up blocked by CAPTCHA

assertions.title: Page loads, URL param pre-fills input, auto-runs check

steps.expected: Page loads (200) with authenticated view showing pre-filled URL and auto-check
steps.observed: Page loads (200) but renders SignInPrompt because user is not authenticated. Cannot verify URL pre-fill or auto-check behavior.

steps.action: Click 'Sign in with Google' button to open Clerk sign-in modal
steps.observed: Clerk sign-in/sign-up modal opens correctly with email, password, and 'Continue with Google' options

steps.action: Fill sign-up form with email minh@example.com and password, click Continue
steps.expected: Account created and redirected back to check page
steps.observed: Cloudflare Turnstile CAPTCHA appears ('Verify you are human' checkbox). Continue button stuck in Loading state. Form fields disabled. CAPTCHA token never generated due to automated browser detection.

steps.action: Wait 10 seconds for CAPTCHA to resolve
steps.observed: CAPTCHA remains unresolved. cf-turnstile-response hidden input is empty. Sign-up cannot proceed.

evidence.consoleErrors: @clerk/react: You've passed multiple children components to <SignInButton/>. You can only pass a single child component or text.

assertions.issues: Clerk development mode uses Cloudflare Turnstile CAPTCHA which detects automated browsers and prevents sign-up/sign-in. The cf-turnstile-response hidden input never receives a token. Without authentication, the page renders SignInPrompt instead of the authenticated ExtensionJobChecker component, so URL pre-fill and auto-check cannot be verified via agent-browser. Additionally, there is a Clerk error: SignInButton receives multiple children components (line 24 of page.tsx).

assertions.title: Displays match status, parsed job info, and application history

steps.expected: Auto-check completes and displays status label, parsed fields, and application history
steps.observed: Cannot reach authenticated state due to Turnstile CAPTCHA blocking sign-up. Page shows SignInPrompt instead of check results.

evidence.consoleErrors: @clerk/react: You've passed multiple children components to <SignInButton/>.

assertions.issues: Blocked by same Clerk Turnstile CAPTCHA issue as VAL-FALLBACK-001. The ExtensionJobChecker component (which renders status badges, parsed fields, and application entries) only renders for authenticated, allowlisted users. Code review confirms the component structure exists (STATUS_COPY map with New/Already Applied/Possible Duplicate/Needs Manual Details labels, parsed fields display with Company/Title/Location/Job ID, and Prior Applicants section), but cannot be verified through agent-browser without authentication.

assertions.title: Mark-applied action available from fallback page

steps.action: Navigate to /extension/check?url=... while authenticated and view check results
steps.expected: A 'Mark as Applied' button is present and interactable after check results display
steps.observed: Cannot reach authenticated state due to Turnstile CAPTCHA blocking sign-up. Page shows SignInPrompt instead of check results with mark-applied action.

evidence.consoleErrors: @clerk/react: You've passed multiple children components to <SignInButton/>.

assertions.issues: Blocked by same Clerk Turnstile CAPTCHA issue as VAL-FALLBACK-001. Code review confirms the mark-applied section exists in ExtensionJobChecker: a 'Mark as applied' button with handleMarkApplied handler, optional form fields (company, title, location, job ID, resume version, profile label, notes), save validation, and success/error state rendering. The button is conditionally shown when status !== 'already_applied'. Cannot be verified through agent-browser without authentication.

frictions.description: Initial page load showed a Next.js runtime error overlay: '@clerk/react: You've passed multiple children components to <SignInButton/>'. On subsequent loads the error overlay did not reappear, but the console error persists.
frictions.resolution: This is a bug in src/app/extension/check/page.tsx line 24 where SignInButton wraps a button child. The Clerk component expects a single child or text. This does not block the sign-in flow (the modal still opens) but produces a console error.

blockers.description: Clerk development/keyless mode authentication is blocked by Cloudflare Turnstile CAPTCHA in automated browsers. The Turnstile widget detects agent-browser (headless Chromium) and never generates a token, leaving the cf-turnstile-response input empty and the sign-up/sign-in form in a permanent loading state. No pre-existing test user accounts exist in the Clerk development instance.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Attempted sign-up with minh@example.com via Clerk modal. Turnstile CAPTCHA appeared after filling email and password. Waited 10+ seconds for CAPTCHA to resolve — it did not. Verified cf-turnstile-response hidden input remains empty. This matches the documented known issue from previous validation rounds.

summary: Tested 3 assertions: 0 passed, 0 failed, 3 blocked. All three assertions (VAL-FALLBACK-001, VAL-FALLBACK-003, VAL-FALLBACK-004) are blocked by Clerk Turnstile CAPTCHA preventing automated browser authentication. The page loads successfully (HTTP 200) and renders the SignInPrompt for unauthenticated users, but the authenticated ExtensionJobChecker view — which contains the URL pre-fill, auto-check, status display, parsed fields, application history, and mark-applied functionality — cannot be reached. Code review confirms all required UI elements exist in the component source. A secondary issue was found: SignInButton in page.tsx produces a Clerk console error about multiple children.
